signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

The legal basis for the processing is your consent. Only those third parties to whom Euroleague Properties SA is legally obliged and the companies entrusted with internal management services will have access to the data. You can exercise, at any time, under the terms established by law, your rights of access, rectification or deletion of data, as well as request restrictions on the processing of your personal data, oppose its processing or request the portability of your data, by sending a postal communication to the address indicated above or by writing an e-mail to data.protection.officer@euroleague.net. club.info: A continental basketball giant, Panathinaikos stands as the mightiest force in the EuroLeague since the dawn of the Final Four era in 1988, with an impressive six championships between 1996 and 2011. Its seventh EuroLeague crown came in 2024. The Greens first took charge of Greek basketball with 12 domestic titles over a 17-year stretch between 1967 and 1984. In 1996, Panagiotis Giannakis, Dominique Wilkins and Fragiskos Alvertis came together and secured the first EuroLeague triumph by a Greek team. A second EuroLeague trophy arrived in 2000, led by head coach Zeljko Obradovic. The Greens won it all again in 2002 and beat CSKA Moscow in the 2007 and 2009 championship games. In 2011, EuroLeague MVP and Final Four MVP Dimitris Diamantidis led Panathinaikos to its sixth EuroLeague crown. Panathinaikos returned to the Final Four in 2012, in Obradovic's last season with the Greens. More EuroLeague Playoff appearances and Greek titles followed over the next few seasons. In 2024, Panathinaikos ended a five-year absence from the EuroLeague Playoffs before going on to beat Real Madrid in the title decider in Ergin Ataman’s first season on the bench. The Greens also secured the domestic championship for the first time since 2021. Panathinaikos returned to the Final Four last season and also won the Greek Cup. The goal remains the same this season: adding an eighth star on its jersey.

heroMedia.title: Round 31 MVP: Cedi Osman, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens

content.text:
A career-high performance in a winning effort allowed [Cedi Osman](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/cedi-osman/004088/) of Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens to earn Round 31 MVP honors with a PIR of 36.

Osman led Panathinaikos to a 92-88 home win over Zalgiris Kaunas with 24 points on 5-of-5 two-pointers, 2-of-2 three-pointers and 8-of-10 free throws, adding five rebounds, three assists, two steals and six fouls drawn for a PIR of 36, his highest in 163 EuroLeague career games. Osman earned MVP of the Round honors for the second time, and is Panathinaikos's third player to take the award this season, after Kendrick Nunn (twice) and Kostas Sloukas.

Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz big man [Mamadi Diakite](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/mamadi-diakite/014027/) registered a PIR of 37, the highest in this round, in a 100-94 losing effort against Dubai Basketball. Diakite finished the game with 27 points on 7-of-9 two-pointers, 2-of-6 three-pointers and 7-of-7 free throws, plus 12 rebounds, an assist, a steal and seven fouls drawn. [Isaac Bonga](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/isaac-bonga/006760/) of Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade amassed a PIR of 34 in his team's 82-88 road victory over Virtus Bologna. Bonga had 16 points on 3-of-4 two-pointers, 2-of-4 three-pointers and 4-of-4 free throws, 11 rebounds, five assists, two blocks and six fouls drawn.

[Elijah Bryant](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/elijah-bryant/009846/) of Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv and [Justin Robinson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/justin-robinson/014008/) of Paris Basketball, each with a PIR of 32, complete the list of top individual performances in Round 31. Bryant led Hapoel to a 75-80 road win over FC Barcelona with 21 points, six rebounds, four assists and six fouls drawn. Robinson helped Paris beat LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne 90-81 with 25 points, six rebounds, six assists and six fouls drawn.

### Individual highs: Codi Miller-McIntyre of Zvezda, 12 assists

Diakite was this week's top scorer with 27 points against Dubai. Nunn leads the overall standings at 19.5 points per night.

Diakite and O'Shae Brissett of Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv each pulled down 12 rebounds in Round 31. Nikola Milutinov of Olympiacos Piraeus is the EuroLeague's top rebounder at 7.3 per game.

Codi Miller-McIntyre of Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade was the only player to get double-digits in assists, dishing 12 against Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul. He also leads the overall standings with 7.1 assists per game.

PJ Dozier of Anadolu Efes Istanbul collected four steals in his team's last-second 80-81 road win over FC Bayern Munich. Aleksa Avramovic of Dubai is first at 1.6 steals per night.

Neal Sako of Valencia Basket and Walter Tavares of Real Madrid both had three blocks in this round. Tavares ranks first overall at 2.0 blocks per game.

Six players registered double-doubles in Round 31: Diakite (27 points, 12 rebounds), Bonga (16 points, 11 rebounds), Brissett (10 points, 12 rebounds), Miller-McIntyre (10 points, 12 assists), Milutinov (13 points, 10 rebounds) and Josh Nebo (19 points, 10 rebounds) of EA7 Emporio Armani Milan.

articleMetadata.title: Round 31 MVP: Cedi Osman, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens

heroMedia.title: Mathias Lessort’s emotional return in photos

linkedIds.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Zalgiris Kaunas (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
On Friday night Mathias Lessort made a long-awaited return to the basketball floor. In his first game in nearly 10 months, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens center took center stage in his team’s win over Zalgiris Kaunas.

Ready to go from the tip off, and with support from his family sitting courtside, Lessort looked like his old self. From his basket on game’s opening possession, through his big dunk, energy on the boards, to the celebrations with his teammates, the former all-EuroLeague center was instrumental in helping Panathinaikos beat Zalgiris 92-88.

Check out the photos from Thursday night in Athens.

articleMetadata.title: Mathias Lessort’s emotional return in photos

heroMedia.title: Mathias Lessort returns in style as Panathinaikos downs Zalgiris
heroMedia.summary: The Greens snapped a four-game losing streak

linkedIds.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Zalgiris Kaunas (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Mathias Lessort is finally back! And immediately making a difference.

For the first time since the 2025 Final Four, and nearly 15 months removed from a gruesome leg injury, the former all-EuroLeague center returned to basketball action. In his emotional return, with his family sitting courtside, Lessort was a key cog as Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens snapped a four-game losing skid and downed visiting Zalgiris Kaunas 92-88 on Thursday night.

Cedi Osman scored 24 points on perfect shooting from the field, and Kendrick Nunn netted 22 points. But all eyes were on Lessort, and for all the good reasons.

“He is the heart and soul of this team”, Osman said about his teammate. “We know how much work he put in in this year and a half. We are really happy that he is back. He did a great job today. From the first play, he brought the energy from the beginning and that’s what we were looking for.”

After a lengthy absence, it was not clear how much the big man would be able to contribute. But if this one game was any indication, Lessort will quickly return to being a centerpiece of everything the Greens do.

Lessort started the game, scored after 22 seconds, had a highlight-reel dunk after 90 seconds, and ended up playing 23 minutes and 19 seconds in which he collected 9 points, 3 rebounds, 1 block and a key final-minute assist.

“I am tired, but I am happy”, Lessort told EuroLeague TV interview with his recognizable big smile. “For me, it was the most important to contribute for the team win. It was a crucial moment for us, and we need to start with the wins.”

Not coincidentally, with Lessort the paint, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens looked a bit more like its old self.

“We moved the ball well, and played more comfortable with Mathias,” Coach Ergin Ataman said.

A quick look at the stats tells that Panathinaikos attempted 50 two-pointers, and scored more than 90 points despite attempting only 11 triples. It also outrebounded Zalgiris 30-23 in the final three quarters.

It's okay to interrupt interviews like that 🫂 pic.twitter.com/lb8OzJIz6F

— EuroLeague (@EuroLeague) March 12, 2026

Ataman stressed that the key was the return of Lessort. And the Panathinaikos coach was so confident in his center’s abilities despite a long absence that he did not shy away from making him a big part of the game plan.

“Zalgiris’s most important player is Moses Wright, and we wanted to get him in foul problems early, we planned to attack him from the beginning with Mathias. Because I saw in practice that he is ready to attack, and we know he can attack very well in the low post,” Ataman explained. “[Lessort] made a great job, and finally after a year and a half, we found our regular game, playing inside-outside basketball game.”

Panathinaikos took the lead for good in the second quarter behind Lessort’s effort and Osman’s baskets. Kostas Sloukas scored 8 consecutive points to end the third quarter, then Nunn and Osman extended the margin to 14 points in the fourth.

However, Sylvain Francisco rallied Zalgiris, which cut it to 85-82 entering the final minute. On the ensuing Panathinaikos possession, the ball went inside to Lessort, who showed no sign of his long absence in game’s key moment, finding Juancho Hernangomez alone on the wing, as the Spaniard made his first basket of the night with 45 seconds to go, sealing the outcome.

Lessort was well aware that he would be the story everyone was be talking about after the game, but he stressed that he wanted to make the night about the team. And he succeeded.

“I tried to execute my routine and approach the game like I approached them before I got injured”, Lessort explained. “Obviously, it was a lot of adrenaline, a lot of rush going through my body. But I tried to calm down, tried to tell myself to approach like I approached the game before. I did not want this game to be about me, I wanted it to be about the team and about the win we need to get. I didn’t try to show something, I didn’t try to be the hero, I was just trying to be a teammate and help my team get a win.”

This win improved Panathinaikos's record to 17-14, tied with Zalgiris. But with seven games to go, the head-to-head sweep Panathinaikos now holds over Zalgiris could play a big role in the final standings. And with Lessort back, and likely only to improve as he gets his legs under him, the Greens have everything to once again become the team to beat.

linkedId.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Zalgiris Kaunas (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Mathias Lessort returns in style as Panathinaikos downs Zalgiris
articleMetadata.description:
Mathias Lessort is finally back! And immediately making the difference.

For the first time since the 2025 Final Four weekend, and nearly 15 months removed from a gruesome leg injury, the former all-EuroLeague center returned to basketball action. In his emotional return, with his family sitting courtside, Lessort was a key cog for Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens snapping a four-game losing skid and downing the visiting Zalgiris Kaunas 92-88 on Thursday night.

heroMedia.title: Stats review: Turning up the pressure
heroMedia.summary: Sportradar's Matt Kamalsky dives into the defensive numbers

content.text:
With eight games remaining in the EuroLeague regular season, the early separation found by the teams in the playoff picture has nearly disappeared as the team in ninth place now sits only one game clear of the team in twelfth. While the competitiveness of the league has evolved over time, so too have some of the strategies teams are using to find an edge.

One of the less glaring examples of that is the application of full-court pressure. During the 2015-16 season, the average team sent defenders to guard the ball in the backcourt only 5.1% of the time with many teams only pressing to foul and only three teams pressing on more than 10% of the possessions they faced. This season, 10% is the EuroLeague average with eight teams eclipsing that mark and two teams pressing on over 22% of the possessions they face.

Some of that can be traced to intent. Many of the teams pressing the most a decade ago were hunting deflections to generate turnovers. There was a reluctance for many years to apply pressure beyond half court as a result – it felt risky to give the caliber of guards at this level a running start if things went wrong. However, coaches have found a comfort level using pressure more passively to disrupt tempo, try to get the ball out of certain players' hands in early offense, or simply leverage their depth instead of aggressively trying to force mistakes.

The table above lists the teams picking up full court most frequently this season. For reference, the league average for turnover percentage sits at just over 14% for all possessions. This group represents teams from across the standings, all finding varying degrees of success and implementing their pressure in different ways.

[Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/) and [Valencia Basket](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/valencia-basket/pam/) are the two teams picking up the ball over the midcourt line most frequently this season. While Paris has posted similar defensive efficiency numbers whether pressing or not, Valencia has been harder to score on when pressing, often burning time off the shot clock and only having their press broken for transition opportunities 5% of the time – a tiny, impressive number at any level of basketball. Valencia does a stellar job eliminating those outcomes, largely mitigating the risk that made coaches reluctant to apply pressure a decade ago.

While EuroLeague teams typically don't rely on the wide variety of press schemes often seen at lower levels, an occasional trapping zone still pops up here and there. Even without as many drastic variations, coaches create variety in other ways. Some teams deploy specific defenders to hound the ball without committing a second defender, others focus on disrupting the inbound pass, and some, like [Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade/red/), adjust their pressing frequency from game to game based on their scouting report.

[Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/) is perhaps the best example of a team trapping to force mistakes this season, as they have come up with quite a few deflections springing timely traps off the inbound pass or just over half court. Generating a turnover on around a quarter of the possessions they press is an impressive result that makes a couple of extra run-outs here or there a minor footnote.

As the season begins to wind down, the little things teams are doing continue to add up and figure to play a role in who gets into the field and who keeps home-court advantage.

While the competitiveness of the league has evolved over time, so too have some of the strategies teams are using to find an edge.

heroMedia.title: Olympiacos overcomes key absences to stretch winning streak over Panathinaikos
heroMedia.summary: The Reds swept their regular-season series over the Greens despite both Thomas Walkup and Sasha Vezenkov missing out on the Greek derby

linkedIds.text: Olympiacos Piraeus v Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (2026-03-06T19:15:00.000Z)

content.text:
There are no [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/) players who better personify the team's philosophy under Georgios Bartzokas than [Thomas Walkup](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/thomas-walkup/008855/) and [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/). It's something anyone can see, and the numbers prove it.

Just check their playing time, which reflects their importance to the team: Vezenkov averages a team-high 29:01 minutes per game, while Walkup plays 24:59 minutes – the third-highest mark on the team this season.

Yet the Reds entered one of the biggest derbies in the EuroLeague without Walkup or Vezenkov, both sidelined by injury. On Friday night, Olympiacos demonstrated that the foundations of its next-man-up mentality are too sturdy to crack, grabbing an 86-80 victory against [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/).

"Thomas Walkup, for whoever knows Olympiacos's philosophy, Sasha Vezenkov, and of course [Monte Morris](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/monte-morris/014212/) [who also missed out] with his talent, are very important parts of this puzzle," Bartzokas told EuroLeague TV after the game. "But we have a really good roster. We stuck together and played an important game for us because, as everybody knows, against Panathinaikos it's not just one EuroLeague game. It's even more than this."

content.title: Olympiacos Piraeus-Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
All 10 players who took the floor managed to get their names on the scoreboard, with eight scoring at least 8 points. Despite Panathinaikos putting up sharp defensive effort in a dogfight from start to finish, the hosts recorded 22 assists spread across nine players.

"All the players played about 20 minutes," Bartzokas added. "We had eight players who scored 8 points or more. It was a team effort. The team spirit was important, especially under these circumstances today. We missed Sasha Vezenkov, we missed Thomas Walkup, Monte Morris, and we had to step up as a team."

And step up they did. [Frank Ntilikina](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/frank-ntilikina/006591/) did a great job containing [Kendrick Nunn](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kendrick-nunn/012774/), while [Cory Joseph](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/cory-joseph/014316/) delivered on both ends of the floor. The two Olympiacos guards netted 10 points each, with Joseph also adding 4 assists and 2 rebounds in his best performance so far.

"You really have to give credit to the guys who played tonight because they really stepped up," Nikola Milutinov said. "Missing Sasha and missing Thomas is a really big part of this team. The team is used to them being on the court because they make great decisions. But the guys who played tonight – Frank [Ntilikina], Cory [Joseph] – really stepped up and played an amazing game. We're really grateful to have the team we have."

Olympiacos set the tone through its defense from the start, holding Panathinaikos to just 13 and 16 points, respectively, in the first and second quarters before the Greens outscored the hosts 27-18 in the third quarter, turning the game into a thriller.

It’s all love between @Olympiacos_BC and their fans ✊#RivalrySeries pic.twitter.com/wNzxBV4Uyk

— EuroLeague (@EuroLeague) March 6, 2026

Enter [Tyler Dorsey](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tyler-dorsey/009849/).

Nunn trimmed the deficit to 67-63 from inside, but Dorsey, ice in his veins, drilled one from deep on the other end. [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) responded with a three-point play, but Dorsey pulled up again from beyond the arc and nailed a second straight triple that made it 73-66 with less than five minutes left on the clock.

"I live for fourth-quarter moments. That's why I'm here every night working on my game for these types of shots and moments," Dorsey said after the game.

Olympiacos forced Panathinaikos into 17 turnovers – six more than the Greens' season average – and capitalized by netting 21 points off them. In Walkup's absence, Ntilikina, Joseph and [Shaquielle McKissic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/shaquielle-mckissic/007853/) pressured the ball, each grabbing 2 steals to secure valuable extra possessions in a tight encounter. Ultimately, Olympiacos's grit on both ends proved to be the answer to its key absences, as the Reds grabbed their 11th consecutive victory over Panathinaikos.

More importantly, Bartzokas's side demonstrated that they know how to make the most of their roster's depth – an essential trait for a team looking to go all the way.

linkedId.text: Olympiacos Piraeus v Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (2026-03-06T19:15:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Olympiacos overcomes key absences to stretch winning streak over Panathinaikos
articleMetadata.description: There are no Olympiacos Piraeus players who better personify the team's philosophy under Georgios Bartzokas than Thomas Walkup and Sasha Vezenkov. Despite being without both players in the Greek derby against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens, the Reds grabbed an 86-80 victory.

heroMedia.title: Omer Yurtseven leaves Panathinaikos
heroMedia.summary: He had fallen out of favor with the Greens

content.text:
[Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/) has confirmed the departure of center [Omer Yurtseven](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/omer-yurtseven/005353/).

The 27-year-old saw his role diminish this season as [Richaun Holmes](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/richaun-holmes/014011/) and [Kenneth Faried](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kenneth-faried/011284/) were prioritized by head coach Ergin Ataman, with [Mathias Lessort](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mathias-lessort/003842/) – who is yet to play in 2025-26 – nearing his return from injury.

Thank you Omer Yurtseven! Wishing you all the best on your next chapter!#WeTheGreens #paobcaktor pic.twitter.com/NXf54TP2Ou

— Panathinaikos BC (@Paobcgr) February 28, 2026

Yurtseven joined the Greens ahead of the 2024-25 season and averaged 8.4 points and 4.0 rebounds in 31 appearances as Panathinaikos fell in the Final Four last season. This season, he averaged 6.3 points and 3.5 rebounds in 19 games but had played just nine minutes and 13 seconds since Round 22.

The move could be related to Lessort’s return, with [Coach Ataman stating ahead of Round 29](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/news/pregame-quotes-round-29-el2526/): "Mathias [Lessort] is ready to play, but he has a one-game suspension and we are waiting for the restart after the national team break. I hope he will start playing regular games with the team and practicing normally."

articleMetadata.title: Omer Yurtseven leaves Panathinaikos
articleMetadata.description: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens has confirmed the departure of center Omer Yurtseven.

heroMedia.title: Pulsating Paris poop Panathinaikos’s party as Hayes-Davis debut falls short
heroMedia.summary: The fast-improving French team grabbed a second straight eye-catching road win

linkedIds.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Paris Basketball (2026-02-26T19:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
The last time we saw [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) on a EuroLeague court, he was scoring 23 points in last season’s title decider to help Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul claim the championship crown and earn himself the Final Four MVP award.

Fast forward nine months and one day, and Hayes-Davis was back on the Old Continent, now tasked with relaunching the title quest of a power-packed but inconsistent [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/).

On first glance, that will be a considerable challenge. Hayes-Davis’s debut was ruined by a fully merited road win for [Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/), which led for the last 27 minutes and flirted with a club record in three-point shooting on the way to a 99-104 victory, which was mostly much more comfortable than the final scoreline suggested.

Panathinaikos has now lost seven of its last 11 EuroLeague games, including several setbacks against teams in the bottom half of the standings as Paris followed the recent examples set by Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade, Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv, FC Bayern Munich and EA7 Emporio Armani Milan. If the negative run continues for much longer, Panathinaikos will find itself in that lower half.

content.title: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens-Paris Basketball | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Offensive production during the recent injury absence of [Kendrick Nunn](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kendrick-nunn/012774/) – who also missed this game through illness – was a recurring issue in many of those losses, but on this occasion, the problem was at the other end of the court as Paris roared to its joint second-highest scoring night on the road in the club’s two-season EuroLeague history.

The French team played with its customary terrific tempo right from the opening tip, initially taking control with a 2-10 first-quarter run, scoring 3 of its first 4 three-point attempts and reaching 6-of-10 from deep by the end of the first quarter. And there was apparently nothing that the Greens could do, with the pattern continuing as Paris maintained its run-and-gun, shoot-on-sight policy – when the halftime buzzer arrived, the visitors had splashed 10-of-22 from beyond the arc to race into a 45-58 interval lead.

That scoring pace couldn’t be maintained, but Paris always had enough offense – from a variety of sources – to comfortably hold off the host’s half-hearted resistance until none other than Hayes-Davis delivered a remarkable blitz of 11 points in 2 minutes to spark an improbable late comeback. The debutant even had a chance to win the game, but his three-point attempt inside the final 10 seconds was off the mark – leading to the ejection of home coach Ergin Ataman as he protested a no-call on the shot.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by EuroLeague (@euroleague)

In truth, though, Paris was the better team for the vast majority of the game. As much as the postgame analysis will focus on the Greens’ ongoing shortcomings, the French team deserves a huge amount of credit for a second consecutive eyebrow-raising road win following a 74-85 success at FC Barcelona before the mini-break. And with the last month also containing a home win over Real Madrid and a 2-point loss to Fenerbahce, it’s clear that Francesco Tabellini’s men are heading quickly in the right direction.

“We are doing good, I’m very happy for my players,” Tabellini enthused. “They deserve to get some reward from all the effort they are putting in practices. Tonight they gave a great effort, and despite some tough mistakes at the end of the game, we really deserved the win. It was a nice one.

“It was important to stick to our identity. All the time we were aggressive, we created problems, and when we sped up the tempo of the game, we were efficient. It’s up to us to dictate and impose the type of game that we want to play.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by EuroLeague (@euroleague)

Once again, Paris’s offense was led by one of the prime contenders for this season’s Alphonso Ford Top Scorer Trophy, [Nadir Hifi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nadir-hifi/012640/). The 23-year-old Prince of Paris drained 20 points on 4-of-7 three-point shooting, keeping himself at the top of the EuroLeague’s scoring charts.

And, if anything, he sounded a little disappointed that his team’s recent form took so long to arrive.

“We stayed together for 40 minutes, we played our game and were aggressive,” Hifi said. “Now we can look forward to our next games – we keep working and improving. We could [have done] better if we had played like this from the beginning [of the season] and we had been consistent during the year, but we’ve got to live with this.”

In the next few weeks, living with Paris will be quite a challenge for its opponents.

linkedId.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Paris Basketball (2026-02-26T19:45:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Pulsating Paris poop Panathinaikos’s party as Hayes-Davis debut falls short
articleMetadata.description: Paris Basketball earned a 99–104 road win over Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens, leading for the final 27 minutes and flirting with a club mark from deep. Nadir Hifi (20 points, 4‑of‑7 3FG) paced an attack that hit 10 triples by halftime and maintained control despite Nigel Hayes‑Davis’s late 11‑point blitz and a frantic finish that saw Ergin Ataman ejected. The victory follows Paris’s 74–85 triumph at Barcelona and a recent home win over Real Madrid, underscoring the French team’s rapid rise.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

content.title: Olympiacos vs. Panathinaikos preview: The Greek derby

heroMedia.title: Olympiacos vs. Panathinaikos preview: The Greek derby

linkedIds.text: Olympiacos Piraeus v Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (2026-03-06T19:15:00.000Z)

content.text:
Tonight's Greek derby between [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/) and [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/) marks another chapter in one of EuroLeague's most compelling rivalries. What was once a closely contested series – Olympiacos led 8-7 this century after the 2021-22 season in their head-to-head record – has transformed dramatically in recent years. The Reds have won the last 10 consecutive derbies, a streak that includes an 82-87 victory at Telekom Center Athens earlier this season. With such overwhelming recent momentum in their favor, the Reds will be confident heading into Round 30, though the absence of star forward [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/) and defensive pest [Thomas Walkup](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/thomas-walkup/008855/) could provide Panathinaikos with a genuine opportunity to finally break through.

For Panathinaikos, tonight represents a chance to upset the odds and reignite its rivalry with a side that has completely dominated it in recent years. The Greens will take heart from the return of [Mathias Lessort](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mathias-lessort/003842/), who is available for the first time this season and could provide crucial depth in the paint. Center [Kenneth Faried](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kenneth-faried/011284/) and forward [Konstantinos Mitoglou](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/konstantinos-mitoglou/007866/) were interviewed ahead of the game. 

Olympiacos, despite missing Vezenkov, still possesses the championship pedigree and star power to extend its remarkable derby streak. Center [Nikola Milutinov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-milutinov/003941/) and head coach Georgios Bartzokas were also interviewed as the Reds prepare for tonight's big game. As the rivalry's current dominant force, Olympiacos enters as favorites, but Panathinaikos's all-star roster – which now includes last year's Final Four MVP in [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) – gives the Greens their best chance in years to finally dethrone the Reds.

content.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season

heroMedia.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season

content.text:
*EuroLeague & Friends Global* returns for its second episode, once again hosted by four‑time EuroLeague champion Kyle Hines. This time, the show dives into *"Crown Season"*, a new docu-series created by Anadolu Efes Istanbul guard [Shane Larkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/shane-larkin/007200/). Kyle is joined by Larkin along with two of the series’ guest stars: [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/), AS Monaco’s playmaker, and [Mathias Lessort](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mathias-lessort/003842/), Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens’ center.

The conversation explores the insights and untold stories highlighted in the series — from their personal investments to Mathias’s road to recovery, plus a behind‑the‑scenes look at their lives away from basketball. Mathias shares his feeling on how he will feel when he finally plays a EuroLeague home game after a long recovery process The players also reveal a lighter side that fans rarely get to see. Kyle asks the trio how the idea for *Crown Season* came about and how Shane managed to bring it to life.

Mike points out that the EuroLeague has huge growth potential, especially if fans begin following not just their teams, but also the players they admire — regardless of which jersey they wear. The group discusses how the perception of players off the court has evolved, particularly after games, and they share a few funny stories along the way. From cultural differences to adapting to new environments, the conversation is relatable and engaging for any viewer.

Toward the end of the episode, Mike, Kyle, Mathias, and Shane debate the idea of a one‑on‑one contest — who should be invited, who would be the favorites, and what rules the competition should follow. Some of their proposed names are obvious, while others are surprising picks you might not expect. And the question remains: would Mike James volunteer himself… or nominate a Monaco teammate?

It’s rare to see four EuroLeague-winning stars openly discussing the league, the culture, and the players from their unique perspectives — so don’t miss *EuroLeague & Friends Global*!
